skill.name: react-flow-node-ts
skill.description: Create typed React Flow node components with handles, resizer behavior, and Zustand store integration patterns.
skill.summary: Build consistent custom React Flow nodes with TypeScript and Zustand patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks and words in examples as command execution and weak cryptography. Review found only documentation and TypeScript examples in SKILL.md, with no executable code, network access, secret access, or prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The static command-execution findings are false positives. SKILL.md uses inline Markdown backticks and fenced TypeScript examples to show placeholders, file paths, and component patterns; it does not instruct shell execution or include runnable command invocations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown inline code, fenced TSX, fenced TypeScript, and numbered documentation steps. No shell command, process launcher, Ruby code, or user-controlled command input appears in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography findings are false positives. The cited lines describe React Flow node metadata, interface fields, and project file paths; they contain no cryptographic algorithm, hashing API, encryption call, or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review of the cited lines shows React Flow documentation text and file paths only. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any other weak cryptographic usage.

content.value_statement: Custom React Flow nodes can become inconsistent across a canvas application. This skill gives a compact pattern for typed node components, handles, resizing, and Zustand store integration.
actual_capabilities: Defines placeholder naming rules for component names, node type identifiers, and data interfaces.
Shows a React Flow node component pattern with NodeResizer, target handles, and source handles.
Shows a TypeScript data interface pattern for custom React Flow node data.
References Zustand store access for node updates and canvas mode checks.
Lists integration steps for types, component exports, default data, node registration, and menus.
limitations: The repository snapshot only includes SKILL.md, so referenced asset templates are not present in the scanned file structure.
It provides patterns and guidance, not a complete generated React Flow application.
It assumes an existing React Flow project with compatible TypeScript and Zustand conventions.
It does not cover testing, accessibility, styling systems, or advanced graph behaviors.

use_cases.title: Add a Canvas Node
use_cases.description: Create a new typed node component that follows the existing React Flow node shape and handle layout.

use_cases.title: Standardize Node Types
use_cases.description: Align component names, type identifiers, and data interfaces before adding several node variants.

use_cases.title: Wire Store Integration
use_cases.description: Connect a node component to Zustand store actions and canvas mode state using the documented pattern.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Node
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a single custom node.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the react-flow-node-ts skill to design a basic TaskNode. Include the component name, node type, data interface, handles, and integration checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a UI Card
prompt_templates.scenario: Adapt an existing React component into a React Flow node.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the react-flow-node-ts skill to convert my existing status card into a React Flow node. Preserve typed data, add handles, and note store integration points.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Multiple Node Types
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning for a workflow editor feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the react-flow-node-ts skill to plan three custom nodes for an approval workflow. Define names, type identifiers, data fields, handles, and registration steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review Node Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of consistency and integration risks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the react-flow-node-ts skill to review my React Flow node architecture. Check TypeScript data shapes, Zustand usage, node registration, menu integration, and missing defaults.

output_examples.input: Request a new VideoNode for a canvas editor.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan with the VideoNode component name, video-node type identifier, VideoNodeData fields, top and bottom handles, and integration steps.

output_examples.input: Ask how to register a custom node after creating the component.
output_examples.output: A checklist covering type exports, node component exports, store defaults, nodeTypes registration, and menu entries.

output_examples.input: Ask for a review of a partially implemented node.
output_examples.output: A structured review of naming consistency, data typing, handle placement, resizer visibility, store access, and missing canvas registration.

best_practices: Choose stable PascalCase component names and kebab-case node type identifiers before implementation.
Keep node data interfaces explicit and extend Record<string, unknown> as shown in the guide.
Register the node in types, exports, defaults, canvas nodeTypes, and menu surfaces together.
anti_patterns: Do not add a visual node component without updating the TypeScript node type and exports.
Do not hardcode canvas behavior that should come from the shared Zustand store.
Do not create inconsistent node type strings across component, store, menu, and canvas registration.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create custom React Flow node components with TypeScript data types, handles, resizing, and store integration patterns.

faq.question: Does it generate a full React Flow application?
faq.answer: No. It provides node component patterns and integration steps for an existing React Flow project.

faq.question: Does it require Zustand?
faq.answer: The documented pattern references Zustand store access, so it is most useful in projects using a similar store design.

faq.question: Can it support multiple custom node types?
faq.answer: Yes. The naming placeholders and registration checklist can be repeated for each node type.

faq.question: Are template asset files included in the scanned structure?
faq.answer: No. The scanned file structure only lists SKILL.md, although the guide references asset templates.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is documentation only, and the static security detections were false positives.
